action.reasoning: Observation: Training failed immediately due to ValueError in kaiming_normal_ (nonlinearity='gelu' unsupported; only relu/leaky_relu/etc allowed). train.py file also appears truncated at end in workspace (incomplete r2_q line). Latest val R2 good (mean 0.687 with q=0.31) but test r2_q catastrophically negative (~-2e8), causing mean_r2 ~-9e7; other components strong (t=0.60, rad=0.95, precip=0.79). Analysis: Init bug prevents run; generalization gap on q-tendency likely from overfitting/outliers (r2 sensitive to large errors). Root cause is init + possibly insufficient reg/capacity balance. Decision: Rewrite clean model.py (fix kaiming to 'relu', increase dropout=0.15 for gen, adjust to hidden=1152/num_blocks=5), provide complete non-truncated train.py (q_weight=10, WD=5e-4, lr=3e-4, select best on mean_val_r2, 40 epochs, stronger clip=1.0, full metrics logging), keep predict.py. This should enable training and improve test q-r2. Run train then submit_eval on best.pt.
